config: binary funnel + rotation x2 + RESIDUAL (centroid-subtracted codes) + tiling tile=8, Cohere v3 1M x 1024, in-RAM, reps=5
note: Residual wired into the main funnel (--residual). Stacked recall/latency/QPS. KEY: residual is a FREE recall win at full bits (1024b C=1000: 0.9960->0.9986, identical QPS/latency), shifting the whole frontier out past 038 (0.9968@922). Fewer bits is primarily a LATENCY lever in the batched engine (768b cuts p50 10.6->7.3ms; 512b ->4.2ms) - NOT the ~1.8x QPS I projected, because tiling already amortizes the scan and the C-rerank is a real fixed cost (dropping C 1000->500 alone gives +9% QPS). So fewer bits = big latency + recall-per-byte win; smaller C = QPS. Best balanced point I (1024b C=500): 0.9952 @ 963 QPS @ 8.0ms - beats 038 on QPS AND latency at ~same recall.
not_included: adaptive-C (048, marginal in-RAM, matters on disk), carousel serving (039-041, stacks for burst latency), disk hybrid (045, 32x less RAM) - all orthogonal add-ons.
